content.full_text:
Fern Polrrrcel PnacrrcEs CoupussroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-56f,0 o Fax (916) 322-0886
October 29,2004;
Sue Olvera, Election Processing Supervisor
Contra Costa County
Registration-Election Deparfinent
524Main Street
Martinez, C A 945 53 -l I 40
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-O+229
Dear Ms. Olvera:
This letter is in response to your roquest for advice regarrding the campaign
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act,).r
QUESTTON
Contra Costa County is conducting a formation dishict election on February 15,
2005. May the first pre-election campaign statement in connection with the election be
combined with the semi-annual statement covering the period through December 31,
2004?
CONCLUSION
Candidates and committees involved in the February 15 election may file a
combined statement that will satisfu the requiranent to file the semi-annual statement
covering the period through December 31,20A4, and the first pre-election staterne,nt
covering the period through January 1, 2005. A filing schedule containing the filing
deadlines and periods covered is enclosed with this letter.
I Govemment Code sections 81000 - gl}14. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the Califomia Code of Regulations.


File No. A-04-229
Page No. 2
FACTS
Contra Costa County is conducting a formation district election on February 15,
2005. The nomination period for the election opened on October 25,2OM. The first pre-
election campaign disclosure statement in connection with the election is due on
January 6,2005, with a closing date ofJanuary 1,2005. The semi-annual statement for
the last half of 2AO4 is due on Janua4r 3l,2005,with a closing date ofDecernber 31,
2004.
ANALYSIS
During an election, candidates and committees involved in the election are
required to file two campaign statements disclosing contributions received and
expenditrnes made in connection with that elestion. These pne-election campaign
statememts are generally filed 40 days before the election and again l2 dap before the
election. (Section 84200.8.) Semi-annual statements are filed January 3l and July 31,
respectively. (Section 84200.)
There is a mechanism in the Act which permits the combining of statements when
an overlapping reporting p€riod occurs, or the filing of the semi-amrual stateme,nt would
be an additional, unnecessary filing to the two pre-election statements. (Section 84205.)
In most casss, however, pre-election filings menot combined with the serrri-annual
statement covering through Decerrber 31. This is because section S20l S(a) provides that
the "cumulative amounf' of contributions received and expenditures made by candidates
and committees is calculated on a calendar year basis (January I through December 3l).
Section 82018 also provides that the crmrulative amount of contn-butions received and
expenditures made can be calculated over more than one calendar year rmder specified
circumstances, such as when a pre-election statement is filed in one calendar year for an
election held in the following calendar year. (Section S20lS(b).) That is not the case
here-
However, ifthe semi-annual and firstpre-election statelnemts arenot combined,
the semi-annual staternent, covering theperiod through D*emb€r 3l,2W,will be filed
on January 31, 2005, more than three weeks after the deadline for the first pre-election
state,rrent and the first pre-election filing; due on January 6,2D5,will cover a single
day, January 1,2005. Therefore, candidates and committees involved in the February 15,
2005, district formation election may combine the two filinSr. A campaign statement
filing schedule which outlines the due dates for filing statements is einclosed for your
convenience. Contributions received and expenditures made \vill be cumulatod on a
calendar year basis, January l , 2W ttrough December 3l ,20[P., and January 1 , 2005
through December 31, 2OO5 -


275-3772
Enclosure

Page No. 3
If I rnay be of any further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact me at (866)
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By 
Carla Wardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division
